$comment: See ADR-0015 (tooling) and ADR-0019 (shape: dispatchable request model — schema_version, adapter_id, parameters, branch_ref, event_time). $id host is a placeholder identifier (not a locator) pending the name screen (master doc §13). Shape changes require an ADR.
title: Intent contract
description: The validated input to registerIntent — the ONLY channel through which model-generated content reaches the deterministic core (master doc §6.3, §7.2, §7.3; RFC-002 §1). Identity derives exclusively from stable_ids + effect_type + scope; adapter_id, parameters, branch_ref, and event_time are carriers, never identity inputs (conformance-tested at M3), and additionalProperties is closed to keep undeclared members out.

schema_version.description: Payload contract version (ADR-0019). Additive changes do not bump it; removals/renames/semantic changes do, via ADR.

stable_ids.description: Stable upstream business identifiers keyed by identifier type (e.g. order_id, invoice_id, approved_task_id, workflow_command_id, authorization_id). At least one is required — the Intent Registrar rejects intents lacking a stable identifier (§6.1).

scope.description: Serialization scope: one in-flight dispatch per (scope, effect_type) (§7.4 item 3).

adapter_id.description: The configured adapter/destination binding for this effect (ADR-0019, C1 finding B1). NOT an identity input.

parameters.description: Effect payload the adapter builds the destination request from; may be empty. Validated against the adapter's per-effect-type parameter schema before persistence; stored with a canonical digest as evidence. NEVER an input to identity or idempotency-key derivation (RFC-001 §1; conformance-tested).

branch_ref.description: Optional workflow branch-lineage carrier for the commit gate's branch check (RFC-001 §4 check 3). Absent means a vacuous pass.

event_time.description: Optional upstream business time (RFC 3339). Evidence only; never used in control decisions.

authority_ref.description: Reference to the upstream authorization for this effect; freshness/binding is checked deterministically at the commit gate (§7.4 item 4).
